Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Working around LASSERTS for the spinlocks is a bad idea. Your linux kernel tree is seriously broke. A better way needs to be worked out.

Patch Set 2:

Hi James,

On a uniprocessor platform which do not have CONFIG_DEBUG_SPINLOCK enabled (which is true for Raspberry Pi kernel), the spin_is_locked() will always be false. The related codes are in kernel/include/spinlock_up.h. I don't have any other good idea to fix this. And it doesn't seems right to assume that the kernel enables CONFIG_DEBUG_SPINLOCK either.

Do you have any good idea?

Thanks!
Li Xi

Patch Set 2:

Looking at this problem I believe the best solution to be assert_raw_spin_locked(). If it is not defined for your kernel version we could backport it. Look at the latest kernel source tree spinlock_*.h files to see what I mean.

Patch Set 2:

Hi James,

The kernel of Raspberry Pi (3.6.11+) defines assert_raw_spin_locked() and assert_spin_locked() correctly. However the Lustre is not using this API, it calls spin_is_locked() instead, which is false all the time on my kernel. And LASSERT(spin_is_locked(...)) will always produce a LBUG. Yeah, as another solution, we could change the assertions in Lustre to assert_spin_locked() using the APIs befined by kernel. But it needs change in a lot of places anyway, right?
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Wrapping every LASSERT(spin_is_locked(...)) in an ifdef is not an acceptable way to address this. Please define a macro LASSERT_SPIN_IS_LOCKED(...) which does the right thing according to the kernel config.

Patch Set 4:

The best approach would be to back port assert_spin_locked that is present in newer kernels.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

I think we need to split this patch into several parts:

part 1: the uncontroversial uniprocessor code supprt that changes spinlock stuff asserts.
part 2: configure check and code to do or not do other task stack printing based on available in-kernel support
part 3: the configure check to add arm (or even better, more generic other arches support as indicated).
part 4: (not sure if we really need it yet) - additional packed attributes on some structures.

Thanks.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

Hi Oleg,

So, I should split this patch into multiple parts and pushed them seperately, right?

Thanks for the review!

Patch Set 9:

Yes, please split this patch.
The very first one can share change id with this one but the rest would need their own separate change-ids.

The alignment stuff is strange, so we'll need to dig some more, that's why it's good to have unrelated changes in separate patches - we can land those that have no objectiosn while working on improving hte objections independently.

parents.subject: LU-4917 lfsck: dump layout lfsck correctly

commit.subject: LU-4199 libcfs: remove assertion of spin_is_locked()
commit.message:
LU-4199 libcfs: remove assertion of spin_is_locked()

spin_is_locked() is always false when the platform is
uniprocessor and CONFIG_DEBUG_SPINLOCK is not enabled.
This patch replaces its assertion by assert_spin_locked().
